name: What's Your Decision-Making Style?
description: Discover how you naturally approach important choices and judgments

rational.title: The Analytical Strategist
rational.description: You make decisions through careful analysis of data and logical evaluation of options. Your strength is creating systematic frameworks to assess alternatives objectively.
rational.strengths: Thoroughness, objectivity, strategic thinking, risk assessment
rational.challenges: Can be slow to decide; may overlook intuitive factors

intuitive.title: The Gut-Trusting Visionary
intuitive.description: You rely on instinct and pattern recognition when making choices. Your strength is synthesizing information quickly and making bold decisions without excessive deliberation.
intuitive.challenges: May overlook important details or data

collaborative.title: The Consensus Builder
collaborative.description: You make decisions through consultation and team input. Your strength is creating buy-in and considering multiple perspectives before choosing a direction.
collaborative.challenges: May sacrifice optimal choices for group harmony

pragmatic.title: The Action-Oriented Problem Solver
pragmatic.description: You make decisions based on practical constraints and immediate actionability. Your strength is cutting through complexity to find workable solutions quickly.

questions.question: When making an important purchase, you:
options: Research specifications and reviews thoroughly
Go with what feels right in the moment
Ask friends/family for recommendations
Choose the most practical option quickly

questions.question: When making an important purchase, you:
options: Research specifications and reviews thoroughly
Go with what feels right in the moment
Ask friends/family for recommendations
Choose the most practical option quickly

questions.question: When faced with a difficult decision, you tend to:
options: Gather and analyze all available data before deciding
Trust your gut feeling and decide quickly
Seek advice from trusted friends or mentors
Opt for the solution that seems most practical

questions.question: When planning a significant life event, you:
options: Meticulously plan every detail using logic and data
Rely on your intuition to guide your decisions
Consult with family and friends to get their input
Choose the option that minimizes risks and is most practical

questions.question: When choosing a career path, you are most influenced by:
options: Logical analysis of the pros and cons
A strong, intuitive sense of passion
Advice from mentors and peers
Practical considerations and stability

questions.question: When organizing a team project, you prefer to:
options: Set clear, data-driven objectives from the start
Encourage creative, intuitive brainstorming
Facilitate discussions to gather everyone’s input
Implement a structured plan to achieve practical results

questions.question: When deciding on a vacation destination, you:
options: Research all the facts and details about each location
Choose based on a spontaneous, intuitive impulse
Ask friends for their personal travel experiences
Pick the destination that offers the most convenience and value

questions.question: When confronted with a sudden change of plans, you:
options: Evaluate the situation logically and adjust your strategy
Rely on your gut reaction and adapt immediately
Discuss new options with those around you
Quickly implement the most straightforward, practical solution

questions.question: When making financial decisions, you:
options: Analyze all the numbers and trends carefully
Rely on your instinct about what feels right
Consult with financial advisors or trusted peers
Opt for the option that offers the best tangible benefits

questions.question: When encountering conflicting advice, you:
options: Weigh the evidence logically to decide what's best
Trust your personal instincts over external opinions
Consider the views of those you respect and collaborate with
Choose the solution that appears most practical and direct
